title: Seeing double with the Artemis 2 rocket boosters | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
summary: The Artemis 2 mission's rocket boosters are shown mid-air after separation in a space photo from May 26, 2026. This highlights a critical phase of the rocket's ascent.

title: Could a cosmic uncertainty principle help explain dark matter?
summary: A new theory suggests that a cosmic uncertainty principle could explain dark matter and dark energy without requiring new physics. This concept draws an analogy to Heisenberg's uncertainty principle in quantum mechanics.

title: Unusual red northern lights over Japan suggest some solar storms are stronger than we thought
summary: Researchers found that unusual red northern lights over Japan extended hundreds of miles higher into Earth's atmosphere than expected, challenging assumptions about the strength of geomagnetic storms.

title: HBO's 'Lanterns' does have some green in it after all, as latest trailer shows Hal Jordan unleashing the power of the ring (video)
summary: HBO's 'Lanterns' trailer features Hal Jordan wielding the power of the Green Lantern ring, showcasing the most powerful weapon in the universe. The trailer highlights the iconic superhero's abilities in the upcoming series.

title: 'Very interesting wiggles' in data from silent NASA Mars spacecraft lead to unexpected solar wind discovery
summary: Data from NASA's MAVEN spacecraft revealed unexpected solar wind effects on Mars, showing how solar storms influence planets without strong magnetic fields. This discovery highlights new insights into atmospheric interactions in space.

title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
summary: DARPA is preparing a robotic satellite designed for deep-space repairs to launch in 2026, aiming to extend the lifespan of geosynchronous satellites. The mission could launch as early as this summer to demonstrate the technology.

title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' wastes a potentially brilliant era of 'Star Wars'
summary: The latest 'Star Wars' film, 'The Mandalorian and Grogu,' is criticized for failing to capitalize on a promising era of the franchise. The movie is seen as getting lost in less engaging parts of the galaxy.

title: Scientists just found a supercharged supernova — powered up by a magnetic star corpse
summary: Scientists discovered a supercharged supernova powered by a magnetar using NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray spacecraft. This rare event highlights the extreme energy released when a massive star collapses into a highly magnetic dead star.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite missions leading the manifest. The launches highlight the growing activity in commercial space operations.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to China's Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 5, 2022. This mission marks another step in China's ongoing efforts to maintain and expand its orbital outpost.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission marked a significant milestone with impressive performance during its first flight.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the coming week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C flight.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The first stage of Starship, the Super Heavy booster, has undergone multiple iterations, with the latest being the Super Heavy Block 3. This new version is positioned as the future of the booster design.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning and processing Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key tests for the Human Landing System (HLS).

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 and expanded its operations by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. This initiative supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are contributing to ESA's missions by testing and ensuring materials can withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to assemble spacecraft successfully.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive technology, likely related to space industry applications. The image accompanying the text reinforces the concept of robustness and precision.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or bake-out procedure is mentioned. The specific details are not provided in the text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination on Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement since shifting to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Launches Starship V3 for the First Time
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3 rocket for the first time, marking its twelfth test flight and introducing a full suite of upgrades.

title: SEOPS Will Start Waymaker LEO Rideshares In 2028
summary: SEOPS is planning to launch Waymaker LEO rideshares in 2028 to accommodate time-sensitive or non-standard payloads that existing rideshare options cannot support.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The U.S. Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing full capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, arguing that current investments are insufficient despite recent increases. The call highlights the need for greater financial commitment to sustain and expand the continent's space economy.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey revealed that improved moon technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey was conducted two years ago to guide NASA's future technology development.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. This initiative demonstrates ESA's commitment to affordable yet impactful scientific exploration.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system will accelerate the nuclear arms race rather than protect America. The system is intended to serve as a defensive measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over collaboration with China in space-related scientific projects.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March to lead the company's ground technology advancements amid a dynamic market. Kuppanna emphasized the future of cloud-native and interoperable ground tech solutions.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched operational space domain awareness software named Borealis, six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness mission. This marks Rocket Lab's entry into GEO satellite manufacturing.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via its L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth round to fund its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development and deployment efforts.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to find innovative solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of satellite imagery and geospatial information.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial IPO documents, providing the first public glimpse into its financials ahead of a planned public offering. The S-1 filing reveals key details about the company's revenue, profitability, and growth trajectory.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily impact the growing space sector.
